volumeTitle: Birds. Lysistrata. Women at the Thesmophoria
label: Birds (Section 1398 - Section 1.1495b)

sections.label: Section 1398
sections.original: νὴ τὸν Δίʼ ἦ ʼγώ σου καταπαύσω τὰς πνοάς .
sections.translation: By Zeus ! I ' ll cut your breath short . He picks up a pair of wings and begins trying to stop Cinesias ' mouth with them .

sections.label: Section 1399
sections.original: τοτὲ μὲν νοτίαν στείχων πρὸς ὁδόν ,
sections.translation: ... now rushing along the tracks of Notus , now nearing Boreas

sections.label: Section 1400
sections.original: τοτὲ δʼ αὖ βορέᾳ σῶμα πελάζων
sections.translation: across the infinite wastes of the ether .

sections.label: Section 1401
sections.original: χαρίεντά γʼ ὦ πρεσβῦτʼ ἐσοφίσω καὶ σοφά .
sections.translation: Ah ! old man , that ' s a pretty and clever idea truly !

sections.label: Section 1402
sections.original: οὐ γὰρ σὺ χαίρεις πτεροδόνητος γενόμενος ;
sections.translation: What ! are you not delighted to be cleaving the air ?

sections.label: Section 1403
sections.translation: To treat a dithyrambic poet , for whom the tribes dispute with each other , in this style !

sections.label: Section 1405
sections.original: βούλει διδάσκειν καὶ παρʼ ἡμῖν οὖν μένων
sections.translation: Will you stay with us and form a chorus of winged birds as slender as Leotrophides for the Cecropid tribe ?

sections.label: Section 1407b
sections.translation: You are making game of me , that ' s clear ; but know that I shall never leave you in peace if I do not have wings wherewith to traverse the air .

sections.label: Section 1410
sections.translation: What are these birds with downy feathers , who look so pitiable to me ? Tell me , oh swallow with the long dappled wings .

sections.label: Section 1412
sections.original: τουτὶ τὸ κακὸν οὐ φαῦλον ἐξεγρήγορεν .
sections.translation: Oh ! it ' s a regular invasion that threatens us . Here comes another one , humming along .

sections.label: Section 1415
sections.translation: Swallow with the long dappled wings , once more I summon you .

sections.label: Section 1416
sections.original: ἐς θοἰμάτιον τὸ σκόλιον ᾄδειν μοι δοκεῖ ,
sections.translation: It ' s his cloak I believe he ' s addressing ; it stands in great need of the swallows ' return .

sections.label: Section 1418
sections.original: τίς ὁ πτερῶν δεῦρʼ ἐστὶ τοὺς ἀφικνουμένους ;
sections.translation: Where is he who gives out wings to all comers ?

sections.label: Section 1419
sections.original: ὁδὶ πάρεστιν · ἀλλʼ ὅτου δεῖ χρὴ λέγειν .
sections.translation: Here I am , but you must tell me for what purpose you want them .

sections.label: Section 1420
sections.original: πτερῶν πτερῶν δεῖ · μὴ πύθῃ τὸ δεύτερον .
sections.translation: Ask no questions . I want wings , and wings I must have .

sections.label: Section 1421
sections.translation: Do you want to fly straight to Pellene ?

sections.label: Section 1422
sections.original: μὰ Δίʼ ἀλλὰ κλητήρ εἰμι νησιωτικὸς
sections.translation: I ? Why , I am an accuser of the islands , an informer ...

sections.label: Section 1423b
sections.translation: A fine trade , truly !

sections.label: Section 1424
sections.original: καὶ πραγματοδίφης . εἶτα δέομαι πτερὰ λαβὼν
sections.translation: ... a hatcher of lawsuits . Hence I have great need of wings

sections.label: Section 1425
sections.translation: to prowl round the cities and drag them before justice .

sections.label: Section 1426
sections.translation: Would you do this better if you had wings ?

sections.label: Section 1427
sections.original: μὰ Δίʼ ἀλλʼ ἵνʼ οἱ λῃσταί τε μὴ λυπῶσί με ,
sections.translation: No , but I should no longer fear the pirates ; I should return with the cranes , loaded with a supply of lawsuits by way of ballast .

sections.label: Section 1430
sections.original: τουτὶ γὰρ ἐργάζει σὺ τοὔργον ; εἰπέ μοι ,
sections.translation: So it seems , despite all your youthful vigor , you make it your trade to denounce strangers ?

sections.label: Section 1432
sections.original: τί γὰρ πάθω ; σκάπτειν γὰρ οὐκ ἐπίσταμαι .
sections.translation: Well , and why not ? I don ' t know how to dig .

sections.label: Section 1433
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἔστιν ἕτερα νὴ Δίʼ ἔργα σώφρονα ,
sections.translation: But , by Zeus ! there are

sections.label: Section 1435
sections.original: ἐκ τοῦ δικαίου μᾶλλον ἢ δικορραφεῖν .
sections.translation: honest ways of gaining a living at your age without all this infamous trickery .

sections.label: Section 1436
sections.original: ὦ δαιμόνιε μὴ νουθέτει μʼ ἀλλὰ πτέρου .
sections.translation: My friend , I am asking you for wings , not for words .

sections.label: Section 1437
sections.translation: It ' s just my words that gives you wings .

sections.label: Section 1437b
sections.translation: And how can you give a man wings with your words ?

sections.label: Section 1438b
sections.translation: They all start this way .

sections.label: Section 1439b
sections.translation: How ?

sections.label: Section 1439c
sections.translation: Have you not often heard

sections.label: Section 1440
sections.translation: the father say to young men in the barbers ' shops , It ' s astonishing how Diitrephes ' advice has made my son fly to horse-riding . — Mine , says another , has

sections.label: Section 1445
sections.translation: flown towards tragic poetry on the wings of his imagination .

sections.label: Section 1446
sections.translation: So that words give wings ?

sections.label: Section 1446b
sections.translation: Undoubtedly ; words give wings to the mind and make a man soar to heaven . Thus I hope that my wise words will give you wings

sections.label: Section 1450
sections.translation: to fly to some less degrading trade .

sections.label: Section 1450b
sections.translation: But I do not want to .

sections.label: Section 1451
sections.translation: What do you reckon on doing then ?

sections.label: Section 1451b
sections.translation: I won ' t belie my breeding ; from generation to generation we have lived by informing . Quick , therefore , give me quickly some light , swift hawk or kestrel wings ,

sections.label: Section 1455
sections.translation: so that I may summon the islanders , sustain the accusation here , and haste back there again on flying pinions .

sections.label: Section 1456b
sections.translation: I see . In this way the stranger will be condemned even before he appears .

sections.label: Section 1458b
sections.translation: That ' s just it .

sections.label: Section 1459
sections.original: κἄπειθʼ ὁ μὲν πλεῖ δεῦρο , σὺ δʼ ἐκεῖσʼ αὖ πέτει
sections.translation: And while he is on his way here by sea , you will be flying to the islands

sections.label: Section 1460
sections.translation: to despoil him of his property .

sections.label: Section 1460b
sections.translation: You ' ve hit it , precisely ; I must whirl hither and thither like a perfect humming-top .

sections.label: Section 1461b
sections.translation: I catch the idea . Wait , I ' ve got some fine Corcyraean wings . How do you like them ?

sections.label: Section 1464
sections.translation: Oh ! woe is me ! Why , it ' s a whip !

sections.label: Section 1464b
sections.translation: No , no ; these are the wings ,

sections.label: Section 1465
sections.translation: I tell you , that make the top spin .

sections.label: Section 1466
sections.translation: Oh ! oh ! oh !

sections.label: Section 1.1470
sections.original: πολλὰ δὴ καὶ καινὰ καὶ θαυμάστʼ
sections.translation: In my ethereal flights I have seen many things new and strange and wondrous beyond belief .

sections.label: Section 1.1475
sections.translation: There is a tree called Cleonymus belonging to an unknown species ; it has no heart , is good for nothing and is as tall as it is cowardly . In springtime it shoots forth calumnies instead of buds and

sections.label: Section 1.1480
sections.translation: in autumn it strews the ground with bucklers in place of leaves .

sections.label: Section 1.1482
sections.original: ἔστι δʼ αὗ χώρα πρὸς αὐτῷ
sections.translation: Far away in the regions of darkness , where no ray of light ever enters , there is a country ,

sections.label: Section 1.1485
sections.translation: where men sit at the table of the heroes and dwell with them always — except in the evening .

sections.label: Section 1.1490
sections.translation: Should any mortal meet the hero Orestes at night , he would soon be stripped and covered with blows from head to foot .

sections.label: Section 1.1494
sections.original: οἴμοι τάλας , ὁ Ζεὺς ὅπως μή μʼ ὄψεται .
sections.translation: Ah ! by the gods ! if only Zeus does not see me !

sections.label: Section 1.1495
sections.translation: Where is Pisthetaerus ?

sections.label: Section 1.1495b
sections.translation: Ha ! what is this ? A masked man !
